reasoning: The Lean type `ℕ →ᵇ I` (with `I = Icc 0 1`) represents bounded continuous functions from ℕ (with discrete topology, per Mathlib's default instance in `Topology/Order.lean`) to the unit interval. Because ℕ is discrete, every function ℕ → I is continuous, and because I has bounded diameter, every function is automatically bounded; hence `ℕ →ᵇ I` is in natural bijection with `I^ω = [0,1]^ω`. The metric on bounded continuous functions is `dist f g = ⨆ x, dist (f x) (g x)` (see `dist_eq_iSup` in `Mathlib/Topology/ContinuousMap/Bounded/Basic.lean`), which restricted to [0,1]-valued functions is `sup_n |f(n) − g(n)|` — precisely the uniform metric on [0,1]^ω. The induced topology is thus the uniform topology. `LocallyCompactSpace` is Mathlib's standard notion matching Munkres. Hence the Lean signature faithfully captures "$[0,1]^\omega$ is not locally compact in the uniform topology."
